description_long: This object is a compact, side-by-side double-barrel shotgun with a short, sawed-off design. The barrels are metallic, featuring a dark, slightly textured finish, and are aligned horizontally with visible twin muzzles. The stock and foregrip are made of wood, displaying a natural brown color and a smooth, slightly curved ergonomic shape. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses two triggers, indicating the ability to fire each barrel independently. The overall form is robust and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over aesthetics. This type of shotgun is often associated with close-quarters use due to its shortened barrel and lack of a full stock.
description: A compact, side-by-side double-barrel shotgun with a wooden grip and short metallic barrels. The design is utilitarian, featuring a rounded trigger guard and twin muzzles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the wooden grip, metallic barrels, and rounded trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the twin muzzles of the side-by-side barrels and the circular barrel openings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the wooden grip and metallic barrels from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden grip and the ends of the barrels, with the twin barrels clearly visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel with short, parallel barrels and no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard enclosing two triggers, with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
